paper_title: Income and emotional well-being: Evidence for well-being plateauing around $200,000 per year

results.context: 33,391 employed adults aged 18 to 65 living in the United States

results.description: Data-driven optimal income threshold from SSR minimization (between $175k-$250k)
results.context: The optimal threshold is found to be between $175,000 and $250,000. The paper states the threshold is between these two bracket values; $200,000 is used as the round figure.

results.description: Percentage of sample with household income >= $250,000 (slightly more than 9%)
results.context: slightly more than 9% of the sample reported household incomes of $250,000 or higher
